description: Cal, locked in a cell, banters with Coulson and reveals the concoction he imbibed to gain super strength. Simmons and Fitz analyze the drugs, concluding they're dangerously unstable. Cal admits his actions are driven by a desperate need to protect Daisy and his family, but Coulson is suspicious that Jiaying is using him for her own plans.

transcript.line: I'm not a polar bear, Phil. So why is it colder than bloody blue hell down here, huh? And, uh, what's with the, uh... the doodad on my wrist, huh?
That doodad monitors your vitals. I'll get you another blanket if you'd like, or maybe some hot cocoa.
Are you mocking me? Torturing me after I willingly turned myself in?
Torture isn't my thing, Cal, and surrender doesn't really seem like yours. Why did Jiaying send you?
Well, then, you'll never understand what a father will do for his child. Never imagine my pain when SHIELD stole my daughter.
We've been over this. It wasn't S.H.I.E.L.D., it was HYDRA. And didn't you get your vengeance back in the day? My understanding is you single-handedly killed a whole lot of people.
I'm curious how you did it.
Tell me you figured out what drugs Cal took, because my gut's saying everything ever made.

I haven't fully sequenced the formula yet, if only for the multitude of trace drugs found, but my initial list includes anabolic-androgenic steroids, a liver enzyme blocker, various metabolic enhancers, methamphetamines, what appears to be gorilla testosterone, and, no lie, a drop of peppermint. I think it's an ill-conceived attempt at super strength.
Other than cap, every attempt has been ill-conceived. Will he survive it?
A single vial would send any normal man to the hospital. Skye's father took three. He should be dead.
